That's it.

Yeah.

Yeah, exactly.

Yeah, I love that.
Sounds great.
Awesome.

Market, branding, establishing consumer trust to a lot of our software portfolio companies.
Closed on a fund last year, a little over a billion, kind of fully in capital deployment mode.
And yeah, excited to connect and learn a little bit more and happy to answer any questions on us in terms of check size, stage, or anything there.
Yeah, yeah, that's super helpful.
Maybe just one quick one is also like always curious on like kind of what piqued your attention.
And also like if like if it's either like hypothesis driven or or something else or there are any signals, you know, just hearing like firsthand.
Yeah, completely.
So I was actually just looking to see we have like a bunch of notes in an internal CRM to kind of figure out how you guys initially came onto our radar.
But I think it was through a couple of ways.
One was, I want to say, is Madrona in you guys?
Yeah, yeah.
So they're an investor.
Yeah, so we have actually our investment in Reed also has.

Drona, so I want to say they had mentioned you at some point, and I think separately we'd seen like a lot of kind of upticks on LinkedIn and founders that we were talking to, and you guys kind of started to come up in conversations across a couple realms, and then I think realistically kind of probably we're upticking on several metrics across employees and LinkedIn follower count and mentions and all of it.
And so it felt like...
All right, cool.
I'm always curious of like the signals.
I want to learn from it because y'all definitely have good signal trackers because it's just like investors are very well-coordinated.
I'm like, man, like why is tech not using the same signals VCs use because they're pretty on point all the time.
Like they know their timing like really well.

Yeah, the one that we use is called Synaptic, Harmonic's a similar one, people use Brush or SimilarWeb, there's like a bunch of them that all just flash being like, something's happening, reach out.
That's awesome, that's cool.
Well, thank you for reaching out.
Have you given a little bit of background, how much context do you have?
I want to make sure I'm, you know, the things you don't already have context on, yeah.
Yeah, mean, I think fairly familiar with kind of the, from the outside in, story, use case, all of that, but would love to just hear the story from you, and can go from there.
Yeah, that sounds good.
Good.
I think what's been unique for me has been lucky enough to work at a lot of companies that did pretty well, like HashiCorp, ServiceNet, SkillAI, obviously, like DeepRam's doing pretty well.

But I think what was unique in addition to seeing the hyper growth and kind of seeing what worked and didn't work, being like an operator at these faster growing companies and startups and having a background in bigger companies is that I was at SkillAI, their first marketing leader for two years.
And, you know, just seeing the behind the scenes of all these like AI labs come together, you know, when they're training GPT and, you know, and pre-chat GPT era.
Also seeing the behind the scenes of the Waymos of the world, like all of these AI models, all these AI labs, and then throwing together all these conferences.
Like, so like I put together Transform, which was like with Sam Altman, Kevin Scott, like Fei-Fei Li, Andrew Ng, and all of those.
And so like, I almost kind of like learn as a non-technical person from that.
And then at DeepRam, I think.
People don't really see it, but DeepPrim is one of the few players that actually is fully integrated.

There's not that many companies that have their own internal data labeling operations at pretty massive scale for a modality and also have their own R&D teams doing model training, model research, and then they train their own models and deploy their own models and serve their own models and do custom models as well for customers as an API.
And so it's like seeing the behind the scenes of that, like, and I'll connect with us a little bit on why that's relevant.

So then, like, as an operator practitioner, right, I started building these AI workflows, and the whole idea is always like, hey, we always want to take our best people and try to get teams to operate at the same level as your best person, right?
Like, that's it.
Like, and you want to hopefully take your best person and also magnify and amplify their output and impact, right?
Like, so for me, my, it was like, I just want to play with these models and start building workflows and stitching together.
I did that a bunch.
And then I was like, okay, there's something here.
It's a lot of work.
I spent over 1,000 hours doing it.

And I was like, okay, there's a lot of work, but now let me take these people in my team or that I would hire freelancers and like, let me just put them in the loop and say like, now I want you to run this process times 3,000.
And just like, this is what I want you to look out for.
This is how I want to review things.
These are the things I need to check.
Just go like, you know, check in if you need help.
And we did that for six months.
And obviously there's a lot more to it, but TLDR is like 24x traffic.
And, you know, 4x revenue.
And then from there, that led to the workshops.
The workshops led to like 170 people paying 500 bucks for.

it turns out, go.

And we started saying yes and started to scale, and we quickly realized the best way to execute these workflows is through code and that coding agents were getting better and better.
And so why try to abstract like AI workflows, which are really complex, out of code when coding agents are getting better?
It's like, why?
Like coding agents are getting so good that you don't even need to code to build like fully functioning apps.
So we pretty much built our own coding agent framework and then a runtime where to run workflows as code and now Task Hub, orchestration layer, and a learning engine on top of that.
And that's like the underneath the hood that I can show you in a bit.
But the really cool thing here is essentially like...

And so because of that, like, we're just essentially executing, like, we're trying to essentially create the engine that builds the blueprint of how really good work happens and learns from it, you know?

yeah.

mean, we certainly have portfolio companies that I feel like may be good candidates for this, as well as our Disney half certainly has a lot of connectivity kind of across the enterprise level.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Explore potential investment opportunity and partnership between Smash Capital and GrowthX](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX has developed a powerful AI-driven content creation and workflow engine, achieving significant traction with minimal marketing](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=5.0)
  - [The company's unique value proposition combines AI workflows with human expertise, addressing complex knowledge work challenges](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=15.0)
  - [GrowthX is considering a Series B round to scale engineering and platform development, though not actively fundraising yet](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=289.0)
  - [Smash Capital expressed interest in staying connected regarding capitalization and making relevant portfolio introductions](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=620.0)

## Topics

### GrowthX's Background and Evolution

  - [Founded by Marcel Santilli, leveraging experience from companies like HashiCorp, ServiceNow, and SkillAI](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Initially focused on AI workflow creation, evolved into a comprehensive content and knowledge work solution](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=328.0)
  - [Achieved 24x traffic increase and 4x revenue growth in early stages](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=449.0)
  - [Now offering $15,000-$60,000/month execution services for enterprise clients](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)

### Technology and Product Overview

  - [Developed proprietary coding agent framework and workflow runtime](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=758.0)
  - [AI editor with contextual awareness and workflow triggering capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=793.0)
  - [Task Hub orchestration layer and learning engine in development](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=1265.0)
  - [Content OS for deployment team use](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=766.0)

### Client Base and Use Cases

  - [Diverse client portfolio including Allbirds founder's new venture, Engine.com, Webflow, and various e-commerce companies](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=667.0)
  - [Use cases span content creation, product description enrichment, image generation, and complex data analysis](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=689.0)
  - [Ideal clients have a sense of urgency and needs that can't be addressed by internal teams alone](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=411.0)

### Team Structure and Expertise

  - [Split into "build" and "deliver" sides](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=1183.0)
  - [Build team includes 10 principal engineers from top tech companies](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=1224.0)
  - [Deliver team led by industry veterans (e.g., former Apple blog head, TripAdvisor SEO lead)](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=1196.0)

### Financial and Growth Outlook

  - [Investing \~$500k/month in engineering](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=1297.0)
  - [High margin business model (30% cost on service side, 2-3% compute costs)](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=1301.0)
  - [Considering Series B to fully staff engineering lanes and accelerate platform development](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=1314.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Priya to discuss with Smash Capital team about potential portfolio introductions](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=1382.0)
  - [Marcel to provide ideal customer profile information for targeted introductions](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=1411.0)

  - [Potential in-person meeting if Priya visits the Bay Area](https://fathom.video/share/vJ5QyzRixktjbNa9hrtPEj_v7xuTosiD?tab=summary&timestamp=1542.0)
